qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Sparse multipart field indices trigger CPU-bound request parsing stalls
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory patch and regression test target orphaned-file cleanup in the async `fileFilter` callback after malformed multipart input triggers `MISSING_FIELD_NAME`, while this finding targets `appendField(req.body, fieldname, value)` sparse-array CPU amplification in the text-field shaping path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies GHSA-xf7r-hgr6-v32p's vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory fixes incomplete cleanup in Multer's file-upload path when an asynchronous `fileFilter` callback runs after request abortion on malformed multipart input; the surviving session finding is a separate text-field parsing DoS in `append-field` sparse-array conversion.
evidence: The advisory fix commit `739919097dde3921ec31b930e4b9025036fa74ee` adds `if (errorOccured) { appender.removePlaceholder(placeholder); return fileStream.resume() }` inside `lib/make-middleware.js`'s `fileFilter` callback and introduces `test/async-file-filter-orphan.js`, which sends a malformed multipart body with a missing field name and asserts that no orphan files remain in the upload directory.

The only surviving final finding's exploit path is `busboy` text-field handling through `appendField(req.body, fieldname, value)`, using attacker-controlled names like `a[100000000]` and `a[x]` to trigger sparse-array conversion and CPU-bound request parsing stalls.

Session artifacts identify parser availability/cleanup as cleared and accept only the field/object-shaping issue: `artifacts/component_family_checklist.md` marks the cleanup family `cleared`, and `logs/orchestrator_final_response.md` states that `multipart_field_sparse_array_dos` is the surviving materialized finding.
